title: Deterministic Telegram-origin restart routing for post-boot completion
summary: Ensure hot-restart completion routing preserves Telegram origin/channel identity and avoids default startup-session fallback when restart was initiated from Telegram.
details:
## Why this change
When a restart is triggered from Telegram, the current boot flow can route completion/notify output to the default startup session instead of Telegram. This creates wrong-channel confirmations and breaks operator trust. We need deterministic origin-aware routing with no fallback ambiguity.

Source evidence (current files read):
- `src/gateway/lifecycle.ts` defines `RestartContext` and persisted restart metadata used at next boot.
- `src/gateway/boot.ts` hot-restart path currently calls `handleChat(..., restartCtx.previousSessionId || 'boot-startup', ...)`, which allows Telegram-initiated restarts with missing session id to fall back to startup session.

## Exact source edits
1) File: `src/gateway/lifecycle.ts`

Edit A — extend `RestartContext` shape with explicit Telegram origin metadata
- Ensure restart context includes:
  - `previousTelegramChatId?: string`
  - `previousTelegramUserId?: number`
- (These fields are optional for backward compatibility.)

Edit B — persist Telegram origin fields when saving restart context
- In the restart-context persistence object, include:
  - `previousTelegramChatId: sessionContext?.telegramChatId`
  - `previousTelegramUserId: sessionContext?.telegramUserId`
- Preserve existing previous session/channel/origin persistence.

2) File: `src/gateway/boot.ts`

Edit A — import Telegram origin type
- Update session-context import to include `SESSION_ORIGIN_TELEGRAM` alongside existing symbols.

Edit B — replace hardcoded startup routing with resolved context target
- Replace branchy fallback logic with deterministic resolved values:
  - `resolvedChannel = restartContext.previousChannel || 'web'`
  - `resolvedOrigin = restartContext.previousOrigin || SESSION_ORIGIN_API`
  - `resolvedSessionId = restartContext.previousSessionId || (resolvedChannel === 'telegram' ? 'telegram-bootstrap' : 'boot-startup')`
- Build `postRestartContext` from resolved values.

Edit C — restore Telegram identity fields before setting current session context
- If channel/origin indicates Telegram:
  - set `postRestartContext.telegramChatId = restartContext.previousTelegramChatId`
  - set `postRestartContext.telegramUserId = restartContext.previousTelegramUserId`
  - force `postRestartContext.origin = SESSION_ORIGIN_TELEGRAM`
- Expand routing log payload to include telegram identifiers.

## Deterministic behavior after patch
- If restart originates from Telegram and prior session data exists, post-restart completion is always emitted with:
  - `channel='telegram'`
  - `origin=SESSION_ORIGIN_TELEGRAM`
  - restored `telegramChatId` / `telegramUserId`
- If prior session id is missing:
  - Telegram channel resolves to `sessionId='telegram-bootstrap'`
  - Non-Telegram resolves to `sessionId='boot-startup'`
- No path hardcodes `boot-startup` for Telegram completions.
- Non-Telegram restart routing remains unchanged except deterministic defaulting via resolved channel/origin/session id.

## Acceptance tests
1. Telegram-origin restart happy path
   - Trigger restart from Telegram.
   - After reboot, verify boot routing log contains:
     - `channel: 'telegram'`
     - `origin: SESSION_ORIGIN_TELEGRAM`
     - non-empty `telegramChatId` and `telegramUserId` (when present before restart)
   - Verify completion message appears in Telegram chat.

2. Telegram restart with missing `previousSessionId`
   - Omit `previousSessionId` in persisted restart context.
   - Reboot.
   - Verify routing uses `sessionId='telegram-bootstrap'` and still targets Telegram.

3. Web/API-origin restart regression check
   - Trigger restart from web/API.
   - Verify routing uses previous channel/origin; if missing, defaults to:
     - `channel='web'`
     - `origin=SESSION_ORIGIN_API`
     - `sessionId='boot-startup'`

4. Type safety/build
   - Run `npm run build` successfully with added restart-context fields and updated imports.

## Risks and compatibility
- Low risk: changes are scoped to restart-context serialization and boot-time session restoration.
- Backward compatibility: optional new fields (`previousTelegramChatId`, `previousTelegramUserId`) do not break older persisted contexts; undefined values are tolerated.
- Operational risk: if Telegram identifiers were absent at restart-time, routing still correctly marks Telegram origin/channel but may rely on transport defaults for delivery.

affectedFiles.description: Extend and persist restart context metadata for Telegram identity restoration.
Deterministic hot-restart routing resolution and Telegram-origin context restoration.

diffPreview:
- Boot fallback can route Telegram-origin completion to 'boot-startup' when previous session is missing.
+ Introduce resolvedChannel/resolvedOrigin/resolvedSessionId defaults, with Telegram-specific session fallback 'telegram-bootstrap'.
+ Restore telegramChatId/telegramUserId and force SESSION_ORIGIN_TELEGRAM when restart origin/channel is Telegram.
+ Persist Telegram identifiers in restart context so post-restart channel routing is origin-faithful.
estimatedImpact: Eliminates wrong-channel restart confirmations for Telegram-triggered restarts and improves operator trust with deterministic post-restart routing.
executorPrompt: Apply the approved src edits exactly as described in the proposal details for src/gateway/lifecycle.ts and src/gateway/boot.ts. Preserve existing behavior outside restart routing/context handling. Run npm run build, report results, and if successful restart gateway via the normal restart flow.
